title: crocus serotinus subsp. asturicus
intro: Crocus serotinus subsp. asturicus, also known as Asturian crocus, is a subspecies of the Autumn Crocus, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the iris family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that may help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The health benefits of Crocus serotinus subsp. asturicus include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or ointment, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal medicine.
